finding: index.md "Running a workflow (by tool)" table uses the retired per-concern command syntax /assess-security and /assess-performance src/server (and /assess-security for Claude Code). D31 collapsed the surface to the single parameterized /assess <concern>; the same file's own Notes (parameterized command surface) and README/ARCHITECTURE all use /assess <concern>. A newcomer copying the canonical per-tool guide types a command that does not exist.
proposed_action: Rewrite the OpenCode and Claude Code example commands in the by-tool table to the parameterized form: /assess security, /assess performance src/server. Keep /assess-all (it is a real shim).

finding: prompts/ is described as a "reusable prompt library" in README and ARCHITECTURE but has no index/README. A reader cannot tell what the four files are, which are current vs. superseded by the .agents/workflows/ framework, or how to use them; prompts/fix-bar.md opens by referencing RhodyPACT-specific paths (.agents/prompts/reusable/prompt_ipd_reviewer.md) with no orientation.
evidence: README.md:216; ARCHITECTURE.md:25-26; prompts/ (final-release-validation-executable.md, fix-bar.md, modular-release-review-instruction-set-generation-prompt.md, older-general-qaqc-prompt-library.md); prompts/fix-bar.md:3-5
proposed_action: Add a short prompts/README.md: one line per file (what it is, current-vs-historical, relationship to the shipped workflows), and a note that the canonical, maintained versions of these ideas live under .agents/workflows/. Point README/ARCHITECTURE prompts references at it.
